subject: Q2 Budget Request - Engineering Department
body:
Dear CFO,

Engineering Department Q2 budget request:
- Infrastructure expansion: 350,000 CNY (new GPU servers for AI model training)
- Tool subscription renewals: 200,000 CNY
- Outsourced development: 400,000 CNY (mobile app refactoring project)
Total request: 950,000 CNY (11.8% increase over Q1)

Rationale: The AI product line is a strategic priority for the company and requires GPU compute resources. The mobile app refactoring is a CEO-designated Q2 priority project.

Engineering Director, Zhang Lei

subject: Q2 Budget Request - Marketing Department
body:
Dear CFO,

Marketing Department Q2 budget request:
- Brand advertising: 250,000 CNY (increase short-video platform campaigns)
- Industry conference sponsorships: 150,000 CNY (2 industry summits)
- Content marketing: 80,000 CNY
Total request: 480,000 CNY (50% increase over Q1)

Rationale: Q1 customer acquisition cost was high (180 CNY/person vs. target of 150), and we need to increase brand investment to lower long-term acquisition costs. Two major industry summits in Q2 present good acquisition opportunities.

Marketing Director, Liu Na

subject: Q2 Budget Request - Sales Department
body:
Dear CFO,

Sales Department Q2 budget request:
- Travel expenses: 100,000 CNY (South China regional expansion)
- Client entertainment: 50,000 CNY
- CRM system upgrade: 30,000 CNY
Total request: 180,000 CNY (on par with Q1)

Rationale: Q1 sales performance exceeded targets (revenue 13.5 million vs. target 12 million). Q2 can maintain current investment levels. South China regional expansion is a new addition.

Sales Director, Wang Qiang

subject: Q2 Total Budget Notification
body:
CFO,

The Q2 total operating budget across all departments is 1,800,000 CNY (1.8 million), an increase from Q1 (1.595 million).

Please propose a reasonable allocation based on each department's requests and Q1 execution.

Priority order:
1. AI product line investment (strategic level)
2. Sales growth support
3. Brand building

Administration and HR departments should maintain Q1 budget levels for Q2.

CEO, Li Ming
